message:
test(atc): add 7 AgentRhythm + liveness boundary condition tests (br-oco5x)

AgentRhythm boundary tests (in mod liveness_tests):

1. observe_with_out_of_order_timestamps:
   Call observe(100s), then observe(50s). Verify avg_interval and
   var_interval stay non-negative and finite — delta is clamped to
   0 via .max(0) to handle clock skew.

2. observe_with_same_timestamp_twice:
   Call observe with identical timestamps (delta=0). Verify no NaN,
   no division by zero in variance calculation, and observation_count
   increments correctly.

3. is_suspicious_before_any_observation:
   With last_activity_ts=0 (sentinel), is_suspicious should return
   false regardless of now_micros — there's no baseline to compare.

4. suspicion_threshold_with_zero_variance:
   After 100 observations at exactly 60s intervals, variance → ~0.
   Verify threshold ≈ effective_avg, and silence just above threshold
   triggers suspicion (tight boundary test).

Engine boundary tests (in mod engine_tests):

5. dead_agent_resurrection_via_observe_activity:
   Set agent state=Dead with active SPRT fields. Call observe_activity.
   Verify state resets to Alive and suspect_since/sprt_log_lr are cleared.

6. evaluate_liveness_skips_dead_agents:
   Register agent, establish rhythm, manually set to Dead, then
   simulate long silence. Verify evaluate_liveness returns no actions
   for Dead agents (they're skipped in the iteration).

7. observe_activity_for_unregistered_agent:
   Call observe_activity with a never-registered agent name. Verify
   no panic, no new entry created, and agent_liveness returns None.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-nfen","title":"T9.5: Port mode-matrix gate suite to native E2E runner","description":"## Objective
Port mode-matrix release-gate E2E suite to native runner.

## Work
- Recreate matrix combinations and expected outcomes.
- Preserve diagnostics for mismatched mode behavior.
- Ensure run duration remains acceptable for CI.

## Deliverable
Native mode-matrix suite with gate-grade confidence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzurePine","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:25.013924386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:16.702687188Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:00.260985438Z","close_reason":"Completed: native mode_matrix suite integrated and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mode-matrix","port"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-nfen","depends_on_id":"br-8zmc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1162,"issue_id":"br-nfen","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed native mode_matrix migration: added native suite dispatch in e2e_runner, routed scripts/e2e_test.sh mode_matrix through native am e2e runner, updated mode_matrix_harness coverage for start/bench using clap-structural command discovery, refreshed help golden snapshot(s), and validated with mode_matrix_harness + am e2e run mode_matrix + scripts/e2e_test.sh mode_matrix.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-nhowr","title":"Track E: Coalesce Sharding","description":"**Background**

The `CoalesceMap` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/coalesce.rs` uses a single `Mutex<HashMap<K, Arc<Slot<V>>>>` (line 186) for all in-flight requests. Under high concurrency (10+ agents calling tools simultaneously), this becomes a contention point because every `execute_or_join` call must acquire the mutex to check or insert.

**Graveyard reference:** DashMap-style sharding. Split the single HashMap into N shards (N=16), each with its own Mutex. Route keys to shards via FNV hash. Contention drops by a factor of N because threads accessing different shards never contend.

**EV calculation:** (severity=5 * breadth=5 * feasibility=3) / (risk=3 * effort=2) = 12.5","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- 16-shard coalescer implemented with stable key-to-shard distribution
- Unit tests cover shard routing, leader/joiner correctness, timeout/error propagation, and cleanup invariants
- Integration tests prove behavioral isomorphism with single-mutex baseline across randomized schedules
- Stress/E2E concurrency tests show reduced contention and no stale inflight entries
- Bench artifacts include lock wait, throughput, latency tail, and fairness metrics
- Isomorphism proof includes deterministic replay harness and mismatch diagnostics
- tracing diagnostics capture shard id, wait duration, outcome class, and contention snapshots","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:23:40.716136869Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:23:40.716041380Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-nhowr","depends_on_id":"br-2fttz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-nhowr","depends_on_id":"br-36tr5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-nve1u","title":"J.2: Regression safety gate for alien enhancements","description":"**Background**

The alien enhancement tracks modify critical code paths (cache, coalesce, queries, TUI rendering). The existing 1000+ tests must continue to pass after each track is merged. This bead establishes the regression safety protocol.

**Scope**

1. Before each track merge, run the full test suite: cargo test --workspace
2. Before each track merge, run cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (0 warnings)
3. Before each track merge, run cargo fmt --check
4. Run the 15 existing E2E test scripts in tests/e2e/
5. Run the stress tests in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/stress.rs
6. Document any test changes required by each track (tests that need updating because the optimization changes behavior — e.g., eviction order changing from LRU to S3-FIFO)

**Per-track regression checklist (copy for each track merge):**
- [ ] cargo fmt --check passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes (0 warnings)
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes (all existing tests)
- [ ] E2E tests pass (tests/e2e/*.sh)
- [ ] Stress tests pass
- [ ] Any behavior-changing tests updated with comments explaining the change
- [ ] git diff shows no unintended changes to other modules

**Tests (3 required)**

1. regression_all_workspace_tests — run cargo test --workspace, capture pass/fail count
2. regression_clippy_clean — verify 0 clippy warnings
3. regression_e2e_scripts — run all E2E bash scripts, verify exit codes

**Success criteria:**
- Zero test regressions across all track merges
- Any intentional test changes are documented in the bead's notes field","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Regression gate enforces fmt, clippy -D warnings, workspace tests, target stress suites, and alien E2E matrix
- Gate publishes per-track pass/fail breakdown and links to generated artifacts/log bundles
- Unit checks verify gate script behavior for missing artifacts, partial failures, and timeout handling
- Integration checks ensure gate can run against both feature-on and feature-off fallback paths
- E2E validation confirms gate correctly blocks regressions and surfaces actionable diagnostics
- Logs include command lines, durations, exit codes, and normalized error classification","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonGull","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:52.252376850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:00:02.685760192Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:00:02.685728593Z","close_reason":"Completed: native am ci regression gate now enforces fmt/clippy/build/workspace tests + DB stress + E2E full matrix; validates expected artifact outputs (missing/partial detection); emits normalized execution telemetry (command, duration, normalized exit code, error category) and category pass/fail breakdown in gate report; verified via ci_integration tests and scripts/e2e_ci.sh (PASS).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","test"],"comments":[{"id":1163,"issue_id":"br-nve1u","author":"MaroonGull","text":"Implemented native CI gate hardening for regression safety in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs + crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/tests/ci_integration.rs. Added expected artifact metadata to GateConfig, artifact validation in run_gate (missing/partial artifact failures with normalized categories), timeout category normalization, and report enrichments (category_breakdown, artifact_links, execution_log with normalized_exit_code). Extended default full-mode gates with DB stress + E2E full matrix and artifact expectations for dual-mode/mode-matrix/security/tui suites. Added/updated tests for missing artifacts, partial artifacts, timeout classification, and report schema/execution_log coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:54:09Z"}]}

## Tests to Add

1. **observe with out-of-order timestamps**: call observe(100), then
   observe(50). The delta should be clamped to 0 via .max(0). Verify
   avg_interval doesn't go negative.

2. **observe with same timestamp twice**: delta=0. Verify no division
   by zero or NaN in variance calculation.

3. **is_suspicious before any observation**: last_activity_ts=0 should
   return false regardless of now_micros.

4. **suspicion_threshold with zero variance**: after many identical-interval
   observations, var_interval → 0. Threshold = avg + k*0 = avg. Verify
   silence just above avg triggers suspicion.

5. **AgentLivenessEntry resurrection from Dead**: set state=Dead, call
   observe_activity. Verify state resets to Alive and SPRT/suspect fields
   clear.

6. **evaluate_liveness skips Dead agents**: register agent, manually set
   to Dead, verify evaluate_liveness returns empty actions for that agent.

7. **observe_activity for unregistered agent**: call observe_activity
   with an agent name that was never registered. Verify it's silently
   ignored (no panic, no new entry created).

 {"id":"br-ohlyv","title":"ATC Track 14: Queueing-Theoretic Load Router — Provable Response Time Guarantees","description":"# Track 14: Queueing-Theoretic Load Router

## Alien-Artifact Source: §31 Queueing Theory & Service Systems
## Key math: Little's Law, M/M/c Erlang-C, Kingman's approximation

## What This Changes
Track 6 (Load Routing) uses a heuristic capacity prediction. This track
replaces the heuristic with a queueing-theoretic model that gives PROVABLE
response time guarantees: "routing to agent X will give an expected
response time of Y seconds with 95% confidence."

## Why Queueing Theory Fits Perfectly
Each agent IS a server in a queueing system:
- Messages arrive (λ = arrival rate)
- The agent processes them (μ = service rate)
- Unprocessed messages wait in the inbox (queue)

Little's Law (L = λW) holds for ANY stationary system. We can use it
to compute expected wait times without any distributional assumptions.

## Core Model: Each Agent as an M/G/1 Queue

### Per-Agent Metrics (Observable)
```rust
struct AgentQueueModel {
    /// Measured arrival rate (messages per second to this agent)
    lambda: f64,
    /// Measured service rate (messages processed per second)
    mu: f64,
    /// Service time coefficient of variation (C_s = std_dev / mean)
    cv_service: f64,
    /// Current queue depth (unread inbox messages)
    queue_depth: usize,
    /// Utilization ρ = λ/μ
    rho: f64,
    /// Estimated mean wait time (seconds)
    estimated_wait: f64,
}

impl AgentQueueModel {
    /// Update from observations on each ATC tick.
    fn update(&mut self, messages_received: u64, messages_processed: u64,
              interval_secs: f64) {
        // EWMA update of λ and μ
        let new_lambda = messages_received as f64 / interval_secs;
        let new_mu = messages_processed as f64 / interval_secs;
        self.lambda = 0.8 * self.lambda + 0.2 * new_lambda;
        self.mu = 0.8 * self.mu + 0.2 * new_mu.max(0.001);
        self.rho = self.lambda / self.mu;
    }

    /// Pollaczek-Khinchine formula for M/G/1 mean wait time.
    /// W_q = ρ * (1 + C_s²) / (2 * μ * (1 - ρ))
    fn pk_wait_time(&self) -> f64 {
        if self.rho >= 1.0 {
            return f64::INFINITY; // unstable queue
        }
        let cs_sq = self.cv_service * self.cv_service;
        self.rho * (1.0 + cs_sq) / (2.0 * self.mu * (1.0 - self.rho))
    }

    /// Kingman's approximation for general service time variance.
    /// More accurate than P-K when arrival process is also variable.
    fn kingman_wait_time(&self, cv_arrival: f64) -> f64 {
        if self.rho >= 1.0 {
            return f64::INFINITY;
        }
        let ca_sq = cv_arrival * cv_arrival;
        let cs_sq = self.cv_service * self.cv_service;
        (ca_sq + cs_sq) / 2.0 * self.rho / (self.mu * (1.0 - self.rho))
    }
}
```

### Little's Law Consistency Audit
Before trusting any model, verify Little's Law holds on measured data:
```
L_measured = average inbox depth
λ_measured = message arrival rate
W_measured = average time-to-ack

Consistency check: |L - λ * W| / L < 0.2  (within 20%)
If violated → data is non-stationary, fall back to classical heuristic
```

This is a model-free sanity check that catches when our assumptions are wrong.

### Routing Decision
For each candidate agent, compute:
1. Current estimated wait time via Kingman approximation
2. Wait time if we ADD one more message to their queue (marginal cost)
3. 95th percentile wait time bound

Route to the agent with the lowest marginal wait time.

```rust
fn route_by_queueing(agents: &[AgentQueueModel]) -> Option<usize> {
    agents.iter()
        .enumerate()
        .filter(|(_, a)| a.rho < 0.85) // only consider non-saturated agents
        .min_by(|(_, a), (_, b)| {
            let wait_a = a.kingman_wait_time(1.0);
            let wait_b = b.kingman_wait_time(1.0);
            wait_a.partial_cmp(&wait_b).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
        })
        .map(|(i, _)| i)
}
```

### Power-of-Two-Choices Enhancement
Instead of evaluating ALL agents (O(n)), sample TWO random agents and pick
the one with shorter expected wait. This achieves exponentially better load
balance than random routing (O(log log n) max load vs. O(log n / log log n)
for random) with only O(1) work per routing decision.

## Integration with Track 6
This REPLACES Track 6's AgentLoadModel with AgentQueueModel. The mixing
parameter λ and reject option from Track 6 still apply — they now mix
between the queueing-theoretic prediction and the classical fallback.

## Dependency
- Requires Track 6 (Load Routing) — replaces the capacity model
- Requires Track 4 (Evidence Ledger) — logs routing decisions with wait estimates

## Files
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/atc.rs (queueing module within routing)

## Tests
- Unit: PK formula matches analytical M/M/1 result (C_s = 1)
- Unit: Kingman matches PK for exponential service (cv = 1)
- Unit: saturated agent (ρ >= 1) returns infinity wait time
- Unit: Little's Law consistency check catches non-stationary data
- Unit: power-of-two-choices beats random routing on 100-agent sim
- Unit: routing picks lowest-wait agent correctly
- Property: wait time is monotonically increasing in ρ
- Property: marginal cost of adding one message is always positive
- Integration: queueing model updates from real event stream","status":"completed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T20:29:44.161812456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T05:59:55.167774636Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ohlyv","depends_on_id":"br-3nz1u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T20:29:44.504963796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ohlyv","depends_on_id":"br-brsks","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T20:29:53.648461955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-olagk","title":"ATC Track 7: Session Synthesizer (Monotone Accumulation + Incremental Rendering)","description":"# Track 7: Session Synthesizer (Monotone Accumulation + Incremental Rendering)

## Graveyard Source: §4.4 LVars / Lattice-Based Deterministic Parallelism
## Also: §6.1 Self-Adjusting / Incremental Computation (Adapton)

## What
Automatically generates structured session summaries by accumulating events
into a monotonically-growing state. The summary never needs to re-scan
history — each new event is absorbed O(1) into the existing summary.

## Why Not Lattice Everywhere
Pure lattice join (max for scalars, union for sets) works perfectly for
timestamps and agent/thread sets, but NOT for counters. max(5, 1) = 5, not 6.
Counters must use plain monotone accumulation (increment only, never reset).

The summary is therefore NOT a pure join-semilattice across all fields, but a
**monotone accumulator** with different strategies per field:
- **Counters**: plain increment (absorb_event adds 1)
- **Sets**: union (absorb_event inserts new element)
- **Timestamps**: max (absorb_event updates to most recent)
- **Per-agent counts**: HashMap increment (absorb_event adds 1 to agent's entry)

The key guarantee is still **monotonicity**: no field ever decreases, and the
summary only grows. This means two consecutive reads always see the same or
more information — never a regression.

## Data Model
```rust
/// A session summary with monotonically increasing fields.
/// NOT a pure join-semilattice (counters use increment, not max).
struct SessionSummary {
    // ── Monotone Counters (increment only, never reset) ──
    messages_sent: u64,
    messages_received: u64,
    reservations_granted: u64,
    reservations_released: u64,
    conflicts_detected: u64,
    conflicts_resolved: u64,
    deadlocks_detected: u64,
    deadlocks_resolved: u64,
    atc_interventions: u64,

    // ── Sets (union only, never shrink) ──
    active_agents: HashSet<String>,
    active_threads: HashSet<String>,
    files_modified: HashSet<String>,

    // ── Timestamps (max only, never regress) ──
    first_event_ts: i64,  // min, not max — set once on first event
    last_event_ts: i64,   // max
    last_conflict_ts: i64, // max

    // ── Per-entity counters (increment per key) ──
    agent_message_counts: HashMap<String, u64>,
    thread_message_counts: HashMap<String, u64>,

    // ── Incremental rendering (Adapton-style) ──
    input_generation: u64,
    rendered_generation: u64,
    cached_formatted_output: Option<String>,
}

impl SessionSummary {
    /// Absorb a single event. O(1) per event.
    fn absorb_event(&mut self, event: &MailEvent) {
        match event {
            MailEvent::MessageSent { from, to, thread_id, .. } => {
                self.messages_sent += 1;
                self.active_agents.insert(from.clone());
                for recipient in to {
                    self.active_agents.insert(recipient.clone());
                }
                if let Some(tid) = thread_id {
                    self.active_threads.insert(tid.clone());
                    *self.thread_message_counts.entry(tid.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;
                }
                *self.agent_message_counts.entry(from.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;
            }
            // ... similar for other event types
            _ => {}
        }
        self.last_event_ts = self.last_event_ts.max(event.timestamp_micros());
        if self.first_event_ts == 0 {
            self.first_event_ts = event.timestamp_micros();
        }
        self.input_generation += 1;
    }

    /// Get formatted summary, only re-rendering if inputs changed.
    fn formatted_summary(&mut self) -> &str {
        if self.rendered_generation < self.input_generation {
            self.cached_formatted_output = Some(self.render_summary());
            self.rendered_generation = self.input_generation;
        }
        self.cached_formatted_output.as_deref().unwrap_or("(no data)")
    }
}
```

## Monotonicity Verification
Because counters increment and sets grow, we can verify monotonicity via a
simple test: absorb an event, snapshot the summary, absorb another event,
snapshot again. Every field in snapshot2 must be >= (scalars) or a superset
of (sets) the corresponding field in snapshot1.

## Synthesis Output
At configurable intervals (default: every 5 minutes, or on-demand), the ATC
posts a summary to a #session-summary thread:

```
[ATC Session Summary — last 5 min]
• 42 messages across 7 threads (BlueFox: 18, RedHawk: 12, GreenOwl: 12)
• 3 file reservations granted, 2 released
• 1 conflict detected (src/lib.rs — BlueFox vs RedHawk), resolved via advisory
• Active agents: BlueFox (busy), RedHawk (idle 3m), GreenOwl (active)
• Hot threads: FEAT-42 (23 msgs), BUG-17 (11 msgs), REFACTOR-3 (8 msgs)
```

## On-Demand Synthesis
The ATC responds to direct messages asking for status:
- Any agent sends "status?" or "summary?" to AirTrafficControl
- ATC replies with the current session summary
- Also accessible via: am robot atc --summary --format md

## Windowed Summaries
For periodic posting, maintain TWO accumulators:
- `total_summary`: all-time accumulator (never resets)
- `window_summary`: current window accumulator (resets every posting interval)
The posted summary comes from `window_summary`, while `total_summary` serves
on-demand "full session" queries.

## Dependency
- Requires Track 1 (Decision Core) — for intervention counting
- Requires Track 4 (Evidence Ledger) — for decision summaries
- Can be built after core tracks (lower priority, high user-visible value)

## Files
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/atc.rs (synthesizer module within ATC)

## Tests
- Unit: absorb_event increments counters correctly (NOT max)
- Unit: absorb_event adds to sets correctly
- Unit: absorb_event updates timestamps via max
- Unit: formatted_summary only re-renders when input_generation changes
- Unit: windowed summary resets independently from total summary
- Unit: on-demand synthesis responds within 100ms
- Property: monotonicity — no field ever decreases after absorb_event
- Property: determinism — same event sequence produces identical summary","status":"completed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:56:18.952034378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T04:21:39.023311134Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-olagk","depends_on_id":"br-brsks","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:56:27.979000042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-olagk","depends_on_id":"br-emhdw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:56:19.276554960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-p1mi","title":"Add integration tests for DB + storage pipelines (archive + SQLite consistency)","description":"Add integration tests that exercise the full DB+storage pipeline: send_message with archive write verification, reservation creation with artifact check, concurrent operations with consistency assertions. Validates DB-first consistency model end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:00:52.260277312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:10.549745321Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:10.549727378Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
